summary: In evaluating heart donors through TEE, low left ventricular ejection fractions (<20–25%) generally disqualify them due to insufficient functional reserve; thus, marginally acceptable hearts—especially those near these limits—are considered unsuitable for transplantation because they fail core eligibility benchmarks related to pump performance under stress.

Answer choice: B
